title: Configure Image Provenance using ImagePolicyWebhook admission controller

input_row.title: Configure Image Provenance using ImagePolicyWebhook admission controller
input_row.description: Configure Image Provenance for your deployment.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes supports plugging in provenance rules to accept or reject the images in your deployments. You could configure such rules to ensure that only approved images are deployed in the cluster. Impact: You need to regularly maintain your provenance configuration based on container image updates.
input_row.audit: Review the pod definitions in your cluster and verify that image provenance is configured as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and setup image provenance. Default Value: By default, image provenance is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/image-provenance.md 3. https://hub.docker.com/r/dnurmi/anchore-toolbox/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22888
input_row.impact: You need to regularly maintain your provenance configuration based on container image updates.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control requires verification that the ImagePolicyWebhook admission controller is configured and that appropriate image provenance policies are enforced. The presence and configuration of the ImagePolicyWebhook admission controller can be checked via API server flags (Automated), but the actual provenance rules and their appropriateness require subjective review of webhook configuration files and policy logic, which are not exposed via the Kubernetes API. Additionally, the audit requires reviewing pod definitions and ensuring that image provenance is enforced as intended, which involves business logic and policy review not accessible via kubectl or the Kubernetes API.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Access the Kubernetes master node(s) and review the API server startup arguments (e.g., via systemd unit files or manifest files in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/).
2. Verify that the --admission-control or --enable-admission-plugins flag includes ImagePolicyWebhook.
3. Locate the ImagePolicyWebhook configuration file specified by the --admission-control-config-file or --admission-control-config flags.
4. Review the webhook configuration to ensure it is set up to enforce image provenance policies (e.g., only allowing images from approved registries, requiring signed images, etc.).
5. Examine the policy logic and rules to confirm they meet your organization's provenance requirements.
6. Review a sample of pod definitions to ensure that only images compliant with the provenance policy are being deployed.
7. Confirm compliance by ensuring that the webhook is active, correctly configured, and that the policies are effective in practice.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.10 PDF
